body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
<!--- One sentence to describe the purpose of this PR, refer to any linked issues:
#14 -- this will link to issue 14
Closes #2 -- this will automatically close issue 2 on PR merge
-->
This PR adds the `AutoSparseJacobian`, which runs a (mostly) automatic implicit solver with `O(N*c)` memory and time requirements, where `N` is the number of values in each column's state vector and `c` is the number of colors in an optimal column coloring of the tendency derivative matrix. This is in contrast to the `AutoDenseJacobian` that was added in #3827, which runs in `O(N^2)` memory and `O(N^3)` time.

All GPU longruns have been tested [here](https://buildkite.com/clima/climaatmos-gpulongruns/builds/627) with an update of the `AutoSparseJacobian` on every linear solve. With the exception of two examples ("aquaplanet non-equil moisture allsky radiation + 1M microphysics" and "aquaplanet dyamond") everything appears to be stable, though it is very slow because of the frequent Jacobian updates. Once this build is done I will try running another one with an update on every timestep instead of every linear solve.

Huge thank you to @ray-chew for helping with the conceptual understanding for this algorithm. Hopefully the code comments and documentation are able to accurately summarize our many long discussions.

## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->
- Implement the `AutoSparseJacobian` and add relevant documentation
  - Add `SparseMatrixColorings.jl` as a project dependency
  - Add a `SparseJacobian` abstract type for `ManualSparseJacobian` and `AutoSparseJacobian`
  - Provide `use_auto_jacobian` and `auto_jacobian_padding_bands` flags for the config
  - Modify preexisting code and docs for the `AutoDenseJacobian` to ensure consistency with `AutoSparseJacobian`
  - Split off `implicit_temporary_quantities` from `temporary_quantities` to minimize allocations
  - Add useful low-level utilities to `autodiff_utils.jl`
- Implement debugging for Jacobian algorithms
  - Use a `verbose` flag in the `AutoSparseJacobian` to log automatically chosen parameters
  - Print block-wise summary tables that can be used to compare different Jacobians
  - Provide a `debug_jacobian` flag for the config that sets `verbose = true` and prints the summary tables
- Fix a bug in the behavior of comparison operators with dual numbers
  - This also happens to fix GPU compilation of saturation adjustment with dual numbers
- Add tests with `use_auto_jacobian = true` and `debug_jacobian = true` for representative examples in the CI pipeline
  - Add a prognostic EDMF GPU test with `use_dense_jacobian = true` as well, since it can now be compiled
  - Reduce `t_end` for the "AMIP Target EDOnly nonequil" test with sparse autodiff from 30 mins to 10 mins, since using 30 mins causes the job to run for nearly an hour
  - Mark the "baroclinic wave moist check conservation float64" test with sparse autodiff as a soft fail, pending @zhaoyi's fixes to saturation adjustment in `Thermodynamics.jl`

<!---

In the Content, I have included

title: Add AutoSparseJacobian algorithm for implicit solver
